title: Reading: Shopping and Numbers
description: Read a short story about shopping in Tokyo to practice numbers and colors.

blocks.passage:
📖 おかいもの (Okaimono - Shopping)

わたしは とうきょうに います。(Watashi wa Toukyou ni imasu) - I am in Tokyo.
これは ペンです。(Kore wa pen desu) - This is a pen.
あかい ペンです。(Akai pen desu) - It's a red pen.

ペンは ひゃくえんです。(Pen wa hyaku en desu) - The pen is 100 yen.
ノートは さんびゃくえんです。(Nooto wa sanbyaku en desu) - The notebook is 300 yen.

あれは なんですか？(Are wa nan desu ka?) - What is that over there?
あれは かばんです。(Are wa kaban desu) - That is a bag.
あおい かばんです。(Aoi kaban desu) - It's a blue bag.

わたしは ペンと ノートを かいます。(Watashi wa pen to nooto wo kaimasu) - I will buy the pen and the notebook.
よんひゃくえんです。(Yonhyaku en desu) - It is 400 yen.
ありがとう ございます。(Arigatou gozaimasu) - Thank you very much.

questions.q: How much does the notebook cost?
questions.a: 300 Yen (さんびゃくえん - sanbyaku en)

questions.q: What color is the bag over there?

items.explanation: Yes. ペンはひゃくえんです (Pen wa hyaku en desu).

items.statement: The person buys the blue bag.
items.explanation: No, they only buy the pen and notebook.
No, 100 + 300 = 400 yen (よんひゃくえん - yonhyaku en).

blocks.title: Shopping in Japan
blocks.content: Japan is famous for its 100 Yen shops (hyaku en shoppu - like Daiso). You can buy almost anything there for 100 yen! Also, when you hand money to a cashier in Japan, you don't hand it directly to them. Instead, you place it on a small plastic tray provided at the register. Giving and receiving change with both hands is a sign of respect.
